_note: Raw seed material for the CoffeeSpace take-home. This is DATA, not a schema. Model it however you think is right — add fields, drop fields, restructure. Records are intentionally incomplete, contradictory, and uneven (some candidates have recruiter memos, some don't; some have feedback history, some don't). That reflects reality. See /raw for unstructured artifacts, including a full multi-source dossier for one candidate.

client.description: Seed-stage (Series A, ~$14M raised) developer-tools startup. 22 people. Building observability for AI agents.

preferences._note: How this client thinks. Captured by the recruiter over the engagement. This is AI context — it should change how candidates are framed and which concerns get surfaced.
hiring_philosophy: Values ownership over pedigree — a FAANG name is not a plus by itself.
Wants startup builders comfortable with no process and no PM hand-holding.
Cares about WHY a specific person would thrive at Tidalwave, not generic fit.
preferences.response_pattern: Priya usually responds within ~48 hours. Marcus is slower (3–5 days) when heads-down.
past_feedback: "We've interviewed 4 ex-Google engineers for past roles. All were too process-heavy for us."
"I care much more about ownership than FAANG pedigree."
"The failure mode I'm scared of is someone brilliant who freezes without a spec."
"Please stop sending people who've only ever taken tickets."

calibration_set._note: Before reviewing the full pipeline for role_fullstack, the recruiter sent these three candidates as a deliberate spread (a clear strong, a comp-flag, and a stretch-junior) to align the client on the bar. The assignment does not tell you what to do with this — that's intentional.

roles.title: Senior Full-Stack Engineer
must_haves: Has owned a system end to end, not just tickets
nice_to_haves: Comfortable with ambiguity / no PM hand-holding
disqualifiers: Needs visa sponsorship (cannot support right now)
Only big-company experience with no ownership

roles.title: Founding Product Designer
must_haves: Has been the first/only designer somewhere
Strong product thinking, not just visual polish
Can ship in Figma and work directly with engineers
disqualifiers: Agency-only background with no product ownership

candidates.name: Jordan Reyes
candidates.headline: Staff Engineer at Stripe
strengths: Scaled ingestion to 100k req/s at Stripe
concerns: Last 18 months mostly leading, less hands-on coding
Never worked below Series C — unproven in true early stage

comp_expectation.notes: Flexible on base for more equity

why_we_like: Probably the strongest distributed-systems profile we've seen for this role.
Strong backend infra background, good communicator.
concerns: Mostly technical leadership recently — need to verify willingness to be hands-on.
All big-company experience; startup pace is unproven.

feedback_history.author: Marcus Bell
feedback_history.text: Strong technically. Genuinely not convinced he'll enjoy startup pace after 11 years at big cos.

feedback_history.author: Recruiter
feedback_history.text: Fair concern — I'll probe hands-on appetite and startup motivation on the next call.

open_questions: Is he genuinely willing to go back to hands-on IC work?
What's driving the move — is startup pace a pull or just novelty?
His email says remote-only now (see dossier) — does that conflict with the hybrid expectation?

candidates.name: Wei Chen
candidates.headline: Senior Engineer at Notion
strengths: Full-stack: owns features from DB to React
concerns: No early-stage experience
Comp expectation is above our band
May be used to more process than we have

why_we_like: Genuinely excellent full-stack engineer — owns features DB to React.
concerns: Comp expectation (~$240k) is above the band and he says it's firm.
Pure big-company background, no startup time.
recruiter_memo.recommendation: Borderline — only advance if you're willing to stretch comp. Flagging before you invest time.

feedback_history.author: Priya Anand
feedback_history.text: Looks great on paper. Too expensive, and we've been burned by big-co-only folks before.

open_questions: Any flexibility on comp if the equity is strong? (He says no, but worth one more probe.)
Would he actually be happy with near-zero process?

candidates.name: Amara Okafor
candidates.headline: Founding Engineer at Loop (acquired)
strengths: Built Loop's entire backend solo for first year
Just went through an acquisition — knows the full arc
concerns: Has only ever worked at one startup — small sample
Might want a founding/lead title we're not offering

feedback_history.author: Recruiter
feedback_history.text: Holding before submitting — still waiting on her compensation expectations and a confirmed start date.

open_questions: Compensation expectations — not yet provided.
Start date — still wrapping up post-acquisition obligations at Ramp.
Does she want a 'founding'/lead title we're not offering?

candidates.name: Diego Ramos
candidates.headline: Software Engineer at Datadog
concerns: Only ~3 years total experience — likely too junior for 'Senior'
No ownership of a full system yet

feedback_history.author: Marcus Bell
feedback_history.text: Interesting and clearly hungry. Too junior for a senior seat right now, but keep him warm.

open_questions: Is there a more junior role he'd fit, or is this a hard pass for now?
Has he actually owned anything end to end at Datadog, or only shipped within a team?

candidates.name: Sana Iqbal
candidates.headline: Senior Engineer at Coinbase
strengths: Local to SF, happy to be hybrid
concerns: Requires H-1B transfer / sponsorship

events.detail: sponsorship conflicts with role disqualifier — hold before submitting

open_questions: Role explicitly cannot sponsor — is there any path, or is this a hard stop?
Should we even submit her, given the stated disqualifier?

candidates.name: Tom Becker
candidates.headline: Senior Engineer at Salesforce
concerns: 12 years at one large company — no early-stage signal
Likely set in big-company process
No recent TypeScript/front-end work

rejection.notes: Strong engineer but we're worried a 12-year enterprise background won't adapt to our pace. Would reconsider for a more senior IC role later.

feedback_history.author: Marcus Bell
feedback_history.text: Hard pass — exactly the big-company profile we keep saying doesn't work for us. No recent front-end either.

candidates.name: Lena Voss
candidates.headline: Engineering Lead at Vercel
strengths: Has seen both a failed startup and a hypergrowth one
concerns: Currently a lead — confirm she's happy to be hands-on IC again
In high demand, likely multiple offers

comp_expectation.notes: At top of band; cares most about equity + mission

why_we_like: Near-perfect domain + stage fit — failed startup AND a hypergrowth one.
Strong TypeScript + infra; local to SF.
concerns: Resume reads management-track (Eng Lead) but she told me she wants to go back to pure IC — worth confirming directly.
Likely has competing offers; 3-month availability is a real timing risk vs the August target.
recruiter_memo.recommendation: Advance fast and interview now; just go in clear-eyed on timing.

feedback_history.author: Marcus Bell
feedback_history.text: Love her. My only worry is the 3-month start — can we make that work?

feedback_history.author: Recruiter
feedback_history.text: Pushing to see if she can start sooner. Scheduling the HM loop this week.

open_questions: Is she actually willing to drop from Lead back to IC? (Resume says lead; she says IC.)
Can the 3-month start be shortened to hit the August target?
How many competing offers, and what's the timeline pressure?

candidates.name: Hana Kim
candidates.headline: Founding Designer at Mintlify
strengths: Exactly the founding-designer profile we want
Dev-tools audience experience at both companies
concerns: Only one prior 'founding' role
Portfolio is strong but mostly marketing-site work, less core product

why_we_like: Exactly the founding-designer profile — has been the first designer before.
Dev-tools audience experience at both Mintlify and Webflow; codes a little in React.
concerns: Portfolio skews marketing-site over core product — confirm depth on real product work.
Only one prior founding role.
recruiter_memo.recommendation: Advance to portfolio review with Priya.

feedback_history.author: Priya Anand
feedback_history.text: Promising. I want to dig into how much of the Mintlify product she actually drove vs the marketing site.

open_questions: How much of Mintlify's core product did she own vs the marketing site?
Comfort level shipping without any other designers?

candidates.name: Robert Nguyen
candidates.headline: Senior Designer at a design agency
concerns: Agency-only — never owned a live product
NYC-based, role is SF in-office
Unclear product-thinking ability
open_questions: Agency background — has he ever owned a shipped product, or only client deliverables?
Would he relocate to SF? Role is in-office.
Product thinking is unverified — needs a portfolio probe.

candidates.name: Priscilla Adeyemi
candidates.headline: Product Designer at Linear
concerns: Based in Portugal — role wants SF in-office
Has never been a solo/founding designer
Time-zone overlap limited

comp_expectation.notes: Open, but relocation is a hard no

candidates.work_authorization: Not authorized to work in US (would need relocation + visa)

events.detail: location + work auth conflict with in-office role

open_questions: Role wants SF in-office; she says relocation is a hard no. Is there any version of this that works?
She's never been a solo/founding designer — can she handle being the only one?
